sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 2.3g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. fat is basically not the story here.
sections.subtitleText: 2.3g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. fat is basically not the story here.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 73.5g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for grains, the carb quality matters much more than the raw number.
sections.subtitleText: 73.5g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for grains, the carb quality matters much more than the raw number.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 12.5g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein helps, but not enough if the carb side is weak.
sections.subtitleText: 12.5g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein helps, but not enough if the carb side is weak.

sections.narration: everything else here is pretty flat.
sections.subtitleText: everything else here is pretty flat.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: magnesium at 33% daily value, iron at 20% daily value, zinc at 19% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.
sections.subtitleText: magnesium at 33% daily value, iron at 20% daily value, zinc at 19% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: on the plus side: useful staple energy source because that matters because grains are often judged by how practical they are in real diets. usually affordable and scalable because practicality matters for staple foods. works as a meal anchor because a lot of value comes from being easy to build around.

sections.subtitleText: on the plus side: useful staple energy source because that matters because grains are often judged by how practical they are in real diets. usually affordable and scalable because practicality matters for staple foods. works as a meal anchor because a lot of value comes from being easy to build around.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: now the downsides: protein quality is limited because even the better grains usually need pairing. quality can drop fast with processing because the grain category punishes refinement pretty hard. easy to overshoot portions because staple carbs can pile up fast without much friction.

closing.summary: Barley has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best when you want a staple carb that actually brings something useful with it.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want a staple carb that actually brings something useful with it.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
